title: Problem brief: PM lifecycle visibility gap
problem_summary: PMs can see pieces of discovery and delivery in existing tools, but they still lack a single item narrative that explains stages passed, artifacts created, personas served, and downstream readiness.
strategic_fit_summary: This problem supports a challenger posture because it attacks a real gap in incumbent PM and execution tools without requiring ProductOS to out-bundle them on every workflow.
why_this_problem_now: AI-assisted artifact creation is making explanation and trust gaps more visible as output speed rises.
why_this_problem_for_this_segment: Scaleups and enterprise PM organizations feel the cost of fragmented lifecycle explanation because reviews, planning, and governance already span multiple tools and teams.

problem_severity.severity_rationale: The lifecycle explanation gap shows up repeatedly in reviews, planning, and delivery handoffs, and the current evidence spine is already strong.

evidence_refs.justification: The notebook shows that current tools either centralize work or accelerate output, but do not make the full item story auditable.
The mixed competitor set confirms that lifecycle explanation is weaker than planning or execution visibility.
Market dynamics show trust and explainability becoming more important as AI output accelerates.

prioritization.priority_rationale: This is the core problem that unlocks the lifecycle-traceability slice and should block downstream delivery claims until it is explicit.
prioritization.reviewer_handoff: PM should confirm the problem framing is strong enough to unlock PRD and story planning without reopening the research packet.

handoff_readiness_summary: The problem brief carries the current segment, persona, evidence, and opportunity truth strongly enough for bounded downstream execution.
